era_context: Continuation of early Iron Age II. Shalmaneser III is in his first or second year of reign (if accession is 858 BCE, this year is within his reign). In Egypt, the 21st Dynasty continues its decline; the 22nd Dynasty (Libyan) will emerge within a decade. In the Levant, the kingdoms of Israel, Judah, Damascus, Tyre, and Sidon are consolidating power in the post-Davidic era. In China, the Western Zhou is in its later phase. The Mediterranean is seeing increased inter-regional trade and contact.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's early military campaigns (traditional chronology)
events.description: In the early years of his reign, Shalmaneser III conducts military campaigns to consolidate Assyrian control and suppress rebellions in vassal territories. His annals record campaigns against the Aramean states and other western powers. These campaigns establish the pattern of annual military expeditions that will characterize his entire reign and extend Assyrian hegemony into Syria and the Levant.
events.certainty_note: The specific events of individual years in Shalmaneser III's reign are recorded in his annals, but the exact year-by-year sequence depends on the absolute chronology, which has a margin of error of ±2–3 years.

events.title: Continued Geometric pottery development in Greece
events.description: The Geometric period continues with refinement of pottery styles and increasing complexity in settlement patterns. Iron tools are becoming more common, and trade networks are expanding. This period sees the gradual emergence of the polis structure and the beginning of alphabetic writing (though not yet widespread).
events.certainty_note: Aegean chronology for this period is based on pottery typology with ±20–30 year uncertainty.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the year-by-year chronology of Shalmaneser III's campaigns is dependent on the broader Mesopotamian chronological framework, which has alternative proposals (Low Chronology, Ultra-Low Chronology) that would shift all dates.
historiographic_note: For 968 BCE, the historical record is even sparser than for 969 BCE. Only Mesopotamian sources provide any detailed documentation. The Levantine kingdoms are known from later biblical and Assyrian sources, but contemporary documentation for this specific year is absent. Egypt's 21st Dynasty is poorly documented overall. Most of the world's population in 968 BCE left no written records.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's reign and military campaigns
